metrics.narrative: The business problem is clearly articulated across multiple sources: Northport is running manual, paper-intensive tender and procurement processes that create inefficiency and compliance risk. The Next_Step log (2025-12-10) states the Northport CEO 'is interested in the procurement manual processes and Tendering/sourcing,' indicating executive-level pain recognition. Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus (Head of Tender and Contracts, champion) confirmed on the 2025-12-09 Avoma demo call that the current process is 'very manual' and they are 'looking to automate the tendering process end-to-end.' However, no quantified metrics have been provided by the buyer: no baseline cycle time, error rate, cost per tender, or headcount savings target. The Next_Step log (2026-01-05) notes Siti 'has asked for costing estimates to present to CFO for (S2C) option and S2P option in her exco meeting 23rd Jan,' suggesting the CFO discussion may have included ROI, but no record of quantified business case exists. The deal is now escalating to the relevant stakeholder level (7 ports, potential $4.5M+ total value per Next_Step 2026-06-01), but no quantified 7-port value case or savings target has been documented. This is a significant gap: the MMC owner (Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary) will require a quantified business case to approve a consolidated 7-port investment, and the lack of one is a closing risk. Recommendation: build a quantified 7-port ROI model (savings, efficiency gains, compliance improvement) and present it to the owner as part of the re-engagement.

Siti drove the initial evaluation: she initiated the demo request on 2025-11-26 (Next_Step: 'keen to see Zycus demo in person, open to replacing Ramco'), attended the 2025-12-09 Avoma demo call (active participant, asked detailed questions about tender automation and supplier portal), and secured internal Northport management approval to move forward on 2026-01-29 (Next_Step: 'Siti now has approval to move forward with Zycus modules of choice'). She requested costing estimates to present to the CFO on 2026-01-05 (Next_Step), demonstrating access to the Economic Buyer. The Next_Step log (2025-12-10) describes her and her team as 'strongly leaning to Zycus, and anti Ramco,' indicating clear advocacy. The champion is at risk of cooling further: the rep outreach on 2026-07-02 (Next_Step) is awaiting reply, and the deal has escalated to the relevant stakeholder level (7-port consolidation) without clear evidence that Siti remains the champion at that higher level. Agilaan Praemkumar (Lead, Digital Transformation) is a secondary champion: he is named in the Next_Step log (2026-04-06) as the person presenting the competitive pricing comparison to the new Northport CEO, and he attended the 2025-11-25 intro call. However, he has not appeared on any subsequent call. Champion strength is developing-to-weak due to engagement cooling and lack of recent advocacy.

competition.narrative: Three competitors are confirmed: Ramco ERP (incumbent, primary competitor), iValua, and SAP Ariba. Ramco is the incumbent P2P module within Northport's ERP stack and the primary competitive threat. The Next_Step log (2026-01-29) states 'mgmt is looking to retain RAMCO P2P, for short time while their contract expires,' indicating Ramco still has political inertia despite the buyer's dissatisfaction. However, Ramco's competitive position is weakening: the Next_Step log (2025-12-10) describes Siti and her team as 'strongly leaning to Zycus, and anti Ramco,' and an earlier Next_Step entry (date not specified, pre-2025-12-10) notes 'Ramco have cancelled last 2 meetings and have put their P2P renewal at risk,' suggesting the incumbent relationship is deteriorating. Zycus is positioning as an upstream S2P layer that integrates with the relevant stakeholder (not a full replacement), which neutralizes the 'rip and replace' objection. George John confirmed on the 2025-12-09 Avoma demo call that 'Zycus has APIs available to connect to Ramco for master data synchronization and PR/PO integration,' and the 2026-02-11 update call clarified 'Zycus middleware supports integration with any ERP, including Ramco.' iValua and SAP Ariba are named in the Competitors__c field but have not been mentioned in any Next_Step log or Avoma call, suggesting they are on the long-list but not actively engaged. The wedge is clear: Zycus provides end-to-end tender and sourcing automation that Ramco does not offer, integrates with Ramco backend (low disruption), and the incumbent relationship is weakening due to Ramco's poor service. How we win: position Zycus as the strategic S2P layer that solves the tender automation pain while preserving the Ramco ERP investment, and drive urgency around Ramco's service deterioration before they stabilize the relationship.

The Next_Step log (2025-11-26) records that Siti is 'keen to see Zycus demo in person' and is 'open to replacing Ramco,' indicating the incumbent Ramco P2P module is not meeting their needs (Siti described it as 'very manual' on the 2025-12-09 call). The pain is confirmed and well-documented, but no quantified impact (cost, cycle time, error rate) has been provided by the buyer, which is a gap in the business case.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are partially known but not yet initiated, creating timeline risk. The Next_Step log references LOI (Letter of Intent) and BOM (Bill of Materials) / MSA (Master Services Agreement) as the contracting documents: LOI was targeted for 2026-03-31 and BOM/MSA for 2026-04-07 (Next_Step 2026-03-30), but both dates have passed with no confirmation of signature. The 2026-03-30 Next_Step states 'Proposal has limited concessions tied to LOI 31st Mar and full BOM signed by 7th April,' indicating Zycus offered pricing concessions contingent on fast contracting, but the buyer did not meet the deadline. No evidence exists of SOW scoping, legal review, InfoSec review, or ERP/systems integration deep-dive having started. The Products field includes 'Merlin Intake' and integration with the relevant stakeholder is a stated requirement, so implementation scoping and technical validation are prerequisites before contracting can finalize. The Next_Step log (2026-03-17) notes 'Proposal Submitted for S2P,' but no record of a formal SOW or implementation plan exists. The deal is in Formal Evaluation stage (not Vendor Selected or Negotiation), indicating the buyer has not formally moved to contracting phase. Paper process is a knowledge gap: contracting documents are identified but not executed, and no visibility exists into buyer's legal/procurement channel, InfoSec requirements, or contracting timeline. Recommendation: re-engage Agilaan or Siti to confirm current contracting status, identify blockers (legal review, InfoSec, budget approval), and establish a realistic contracting timeline tied to the owner approval conclusion.

economic_buyer.narrative: Two Economic Buyers are identified, but neither has been directly engaged by Zycus on a recorded call, creating significant closing risk. At the Northport level, the CFO holds budget authority: the Next_Step log (2026-01-05) states Siti 'has asked for costing estimates to present to CFO for (S2C) option and S2P option in her exco meeting 23rd Jan,' and the 2026-03-12 entry notes 'internal discussion on next step (with CFO/CIO/CPO).' However, the Northport CFO has never appeared on an Avoma call, and no confirmation exists that the CFO signed off on the $650k Northport investment. At the MMC Group level, the ultimate Economic Buyer is Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary (MMC Group owner), who controls budget for the 7-port consolidation. The Next_Step log (2026-06-01) states 'we are trying to take a proposition directly to the owner,' and the 2026-07-02 entry reports the owner 'received a positive response and level of interest' after the proposal was presented by the relevant stakeholder (an intermediary, not Zycus). However, Zycus has never directly engaged Tan Sri, and commercial sign-off is pending budget tolerance confirmation (1-2 week window per 2026-07-02 Next_Step). The lack of direct Zycus-to-owner engagement is the single largest vulnerability: if the intermediary presentation did not land or if the owner deprioritizes the investment, the deal stalls with no fallback. Economic Buyer engagement is partial and high-risk.

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer's decision criteria are well-documented across calls and Next_Step logs, with strong alignment to Zycus capabilities. Primary criteria: (1) End-to-end tender automation (RFQ, tendering, contract management) to replace manual processes. Siti confirmed on the 2025-12-09 Avoma demo call that they are 'looking to automate the tendering process end-to-end' and want to see 'how Zycus handles tender evaluation, scoring, and award workflows.' (2) Integration with incumbent Ramco ERP for master data synchronization and PR/PO flow. George John confirmed on the 2025-12-09 demo call that 'Zycus has APIs available to connect to Ramco for master data synchronization and PR/PO integration,' and the 2026-02-11 Avoma update call clarified 'Zycus middleware with mapping and orchestration supports integration with any ERP, including Ramco.' (3) Supplier collaboration and portal capabilities (iSupplier). Siti asked on the 2025-12-09 call 'how suppliers interact with the platform for tender submission and document upload,' and George confirmed iSupplier portal functionality. (4) AI/Merlin capabilities for intake and intelligent sourcing. The Products field includes 'Merlin Intake,' and the Next_Step log (2026-06-01) notes 'potential upside additional 7 Ports' with 'added 1% per additional Port,' suggesting AI-driven efficiency is a value lever. (5) Non-tender path: the Next_Step log (2026-04-06) states the new Northport CEO 'only needs to see they did some diligence, given it is not going to tender,' indicating a streamlined, non-RFP procurement process is acceptable. Zycus is well-positioned on all criteria, but the lack of a formal BRD or RFP document means the criteria are inferred from calls and Next_Step rather than a structured buyer-issued document.

Until the owner gives explicit commercial sign-off, the deal cannot close, and there is no fallback if the intermediary presentation did not land or if the owner deprioritizes the investment. This is the single largest vulnerability.

Deal value is $650k for Northport, with potential upside of 7 additional MMC ports (incremental 1% discount per port, potential total ~$1M+ across all 7 ports per Next_Step 2026-06-01, 2026-07-02). However, budget approval sits at the MMC owner level, not Northport level. Internal Northport management approved moving forward with Zycus on 2026-01-29, but the CFO has not been directly engaged on any call, and no confirmation exists that the CFO signed off on the $650k investment. The Next_Step log (2026-04-06) states that the new Northport CEO 'only needs to see they did some diligence' (not formal tender), suggesting the budget authority for Northport-level spend may already be secured, but the MMC owner-level approval for the consolidated 7-port proposition is unconfirmed. Mitigation: build and present the quantified 7-port ROI to the owner via the champion network to convert the 'positive response' into budget sign-off.

Zycus is positioning as an upstream S2P layer that integrates with Ramco backend (not a rip-and-replace), neutralizing disruption concerns. iValua and SAP Ariba are on the long-list but not actively engaged. The wedge is end-to-end tender and sourcing automation (which Ramco does not offer) plus low-disruption Ramco integration. Risk: if Ramco stabilizes their service or if the buyer decides to retain Ramco P2P long-term, Zycus scope may be limited to upstream sourcing only, reducing deal value.

deltas.from: Deal value is $650k for Northport, with potential upside of 7 additional MMC ports (incremental 1% discount per port, potential total ~$4.5M+ across all 7 ports
deltas.subject: Deal value is $650k for Northport, with potential upside of 7 additional MMC ports (incremental 1% discount per port, potential total ~$4.5M+ across all 7 ports per Next_Step 2026-06-01). However, budget approval sits at the MMC owner level (Tan Sri), not Northport level. Internal Northport management approved moving forward with Zycus on 2026-01-29, but the CFO/CIO/CPO have not been directly engaged on any call, and no confirmation exists that the CFO signed off on the $650k investment. The Next_Step log (2026-04-06) states that the new Northport CEO 'only needs to see they did some diligence' (not formal tender), suggesting the budget authority for Northport-level spend may already be secured, but the MMC owner-level approval for the consolidated 7-port proposition is unconfirmed. Budget risk: if the owner presentation does not result in commercial sign-off, the deal stalls despite internal Northport approval.

deltas.to: Close date 2026-08-30 (59 days away) is optimistic given the remaining gates: (1) owner presentation to the MMC Group owner planned for week of 2026-06-08, stat
deltas.subject: Close date 2026-08-30 (59 days away) is optimistic given the remaining gates: (1) owner presentation to the MMC Group owner planned for week of 2026-06-08, status unconfirmed as of 2026-07-02; (2) competitive pricing comparison to be presented to new Northport CEO on ~2026-04-06–07, status unconfirmed; (3) LOI signature targeted 2026-03-31, likely missed; (4) BOM/MSA sign-off targeted 2026-04-07, likely missed; (5) SOW scoping, legal review, InfoSec review, and contracting not yet started. The deal is in Formal Evaluation stage (not yet Negotiation/Vendor Selected), indicating the buyer has not formally moved to contracting phase despite internal approvals at Northport level. Realistically, if the owner presentation concludes in the next 2 weeks and approval follows immediately, 90–120 days remain for contracting and implementation kickoff, putting the realistic close date in Q4 2026 (Oct–Nov 2026), not 2026-08-30. If the owner presentation is delayed or approval takes longer, the deal slips to 2027. Mitigation: re-engage Agilaan/Siti to confirm owner approval timeline and pivot to realistic close date (Q4 2026) to avoid forecast miss.

deltas.from: Integration with incumbent Ramco ERP is a stated requirement and a competitive wedge (Zycus positions as upstream S2P layer integrated with Ramco P2P/ERP backen
deltas.subject: Integration with incumbent Ramco ERP is a stated requirement and a competitive wedge (Zycus positions as upstream S2P layer integrated with Ramco P2P/ERP backend). Zycus demonstrated integration approach on Avoma demo call 2025-12-09 ('APIs available to connect Zycus to Ramco for master data synchronization and PR/PO integration' and 'custom payment integration for tender fee deposits') and confirmed on 2026-02-11 update call ('Zycus middleware with mapping and orchestration supports integration with any ERP, including Ramco'). However, no technical validation or proof-of-integration has been conducted with the relevant stakeholder team (Rosina, IT lead, appeared on 2025-11-25 intro call but did not participate in integration discussion). If Ramco integration is more complex or costly than expected, it could delay implementation or increase scope/cost. Integration risk is moderate (approach is clear, but technical validation not yet done).

deltas.to: The deal is escalating from Northport (one port) to the relevant stakeholder (7 ports), which increases deal size and strategic value but also adds political co
deltas.from: The deal is escalating from Northport (one port) to the relevant stakeholder (7 ports), which increases deal size and strategic value but also adds political co
deltas.subject: The deal is escalating from Northport (one port) to the relevant stakeholder (7 ports), which increases deal size and strategic value but also adds political complexity. The Next_Step log (2026-06-01) states: 'opportunity will move under a new entry for MMC where we are trying to merge 7 ports and take a proposition directly to the owner.' This consolidation may face internal resistance from the other 6 ports if they were not consulted or if they have different procurement systems/preferences. The Avoma catch-up call on 2025-12-30 flagged this: 'the problem with executing that is does the MMC have a strategic person who can push that kind of ecosystem through?' If the other ports resist a shared/consolidated platform, the deal may revert to Northport-only scope, reducing deal value from potential $4.5M+ to $650k. Political risk is moderate (consolidation strategy is ambitious but not yet validated with other MMC ports).
